spec_match.rationale: Builds a rotated `VideoFrame`, pipes it through `VideoEncoder` → `EncodedVideoChunk` → `VideoDecoder` and inspects the decoded frame's `.rotation`/`.flip`. That is the round-trip the chromestatus summary calls out as the spec's whole reason for adding `VideoDecoderConfig.rotation`/`flip`.

interactive.rationale: Numbered step controls (input + select + checkbox + button) build the source frame, kick off the encode/decode, and report each stage in a step log — much further than a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises an encode→decode assertion that orientation survives, citing `encoding may silently encode incorrectly` from the explainer; the page delivers exactly that path with a side-by-side input/output.

edge_cases.rationale: Step log is heavy on failure-mode language (`edge`, `error`, `reject`, `invalid`, `fail` etc — 13 hits) and the explainer-quoted silent-encode-wrong case is the core of the demo. Could still add an explicit DataError demo for changing orientation between frames, but the existing coverage is strong.

references.rationale: `see also` includes the chromestatus link and the W3C WebCodecs VideoFrame section.

design_system.rationale: Mostly uses CSS variables but inline styles still contain raw hex (`#fdfcf8`, `#0022ff`, `#d11a2a`, `#0a8f5c`, `#b87b00`, `#f4f3ee`) for the step log severity tags and headings. CLAUDE.md invariant #5 only permits the variables defined in `public/styles.css`.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex with CSS variables (severity tags + headings)
openQuestions.detail: Inline styles use `#fdfcf8`, `#0022ff`, `#d11a2a`, `#0a8f5c`, `#b87b00`, `#f4f3ee`. Per invariant #5 these should be `--bg-ivory/paper/stone` and `--accent-blue/rose/emerald`. The step-log severity colours look like they were sampled from the design system but pinned in raw — swap them to variables so theming works.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the DataError on orientation change mid-stream
openQuestions.detail: The spec throws `DataError` if a frame's rotation/flip don't match the encoder's `[[active orientation]]` set on the first frame after configure. Add a small `change orientation between frames` toggle that attempts to encode two frames with different rotations and surfaces the resulting DataError — that is the only error path the spec explicitly defines for this feature.

openQuestions.title: Show what the bitstream actually carries
openQuestions.detail: The explainer warning is that the codec bitstream itself may not encode orientation. After the round-trip, surface a small `bitstream container` readout: is the rotation surviving because the codec recorded it, or because the WebCodecs layer applied it on decode? Hex-dumping the first chunk's metadata would close that loop.

summary: This is the headline concept for the feature: it stages the silent-encode-wrong failure mode the spec exists to fix, then runs the actual round-trip to confirm orientation survives. Two follow-ups would round it out — swap inline hex for variables, and add the DataError-on-orientation-change path the spec mandates.
